hand.title: Daily Hand

events.text: Your cards — J, 7. Both players called a raise before the cards were dealt.
Turn — 5. Your opponent led out with a bet. You called.
River — 3. Your opponent fired again — half the pot.

wrongReasons: A 4 is close, but the river lead is the tell. The correct hand picked up more on the river than A 4 would have.
8 5 has little reason to lead the river here — one pair can't comfortably go for value after calling two streets.
8 6 is close, but the river lead is the tell. The correct hand picked up more on the river than 8 6 would have.
hand.explanation:
Answer: K 6

Your opponent flopped a straight and immediately raised to build the pot. They led every remaining street — consistent value betting across all streets is a strong signal of a made hand.
